<tr><td><strong>FE-161</strong></td><td>High</td><td>High</td><td style='max-width:480px;overflow:hidden;text-overflow:ellipsis;white-space:nowrap' title='UI: Create a dedicated &#x27;Affordability Settings&#x27; page — personal mortgage tracker for monthly budget, LTV band, loan term, and deposit. <tr><td><strong>FE-165</strong></td><td>High</td><td>High</td><td style='max-width:480px;overflow:hidden;text-overflow:ellipsis;white-space:nowrap' title='UI: Build &#x27;Market Conditions Radar&#x27; — a Bloomberg-density market intelligence panel giving the user a single-glance verdict on whether the market is in their favour. Four new components: (1) MarketConditionsBar — slim horizontal status strip showing Market Mode (BUYER/NEUTRAL/SELLER from MOS), Negotiation Room (%), Rate Signal (FALLING/HOLDING/RISING from swap rate trend), Seasonal Window score (1-10), and composite Buyer Favour Score (0-10). (2) AreaPerformanceTable — compact table replacing the Area Heat bar chart, showing each target borough&#x27;s 12-month HPI forecast vs London-wide benchmark with delta column. (3) SwapRateSignal — leading mortgage indicator strip showing current 2yr/5yr GBP swap rates, implied fixed mortgage rate, directional signal, and 5-month sparkline history. (4) BoERatePathChart — SVG fan chart showing current rate, market-implied path Q3 2026-Q2 2027 (bear/base/bull scenarios), and MPC meeting markers. Also compact-rework MarketPulse: reduce 4-KPI + heat bars + summary spread across two rows into a dense single-section with SDLT urgency badge. Integrate MarketConditionsBar above the property grid. Add AreaPerformanceTable, SwapRateSignal, and BoERatePathChart below MarketPulse. Extend types/macro.ts with BoERateConsensus, SwapRates, HPIForecasts, AreaTrends interfaces. Extend useMacroData.ts to normalise and expose all new fields. Requires boe_rate_consensus, swap_rates, hpi_forecasts, and area_trends in macro_trend.json (already populated).'>UI: Build &#x27;Market Conditions Radar&#x27; — a Bloomberg-density market intelligence panel giving the user a single-glance verdict on whether the market is in their favour. <tr><td><strong>FE-167</strong></td><td>High</td><td>Low</td><td style='max-width:480px;overflow:hidden;text-overflow:ellipsis;white-space:nowrap' title='UI: Make loan term configurable on the Affordability Settings page. Currently `termYears` is hardcoded to 25 years in `useAffordability.ts` (useState(25)) and the Loan Term panel in `AffordabilitySettings.tsx` shows a static non-interactive placeholder (&#x27;Standard 25-Year Term&#x27;). (1) In `useAffordability.ts`: change `[termYears]` from `useState(25)` to `[termYears, setTermYears]` settable state. Persist `termYears` to localStorage under a `propSearch_loan_term` key. Expose `setTermYears` via an `updateTermYears` callback in the hook return. (2) In `AffordabilitySettings.tsx`: replace the static &#x27;25 Years&#x27; display in the Loan Term section (line 259) with an interactive segmented control or dropdown allowing the user to select from [15, 20, 25, 30] year terms. Wire `updateTermYears` to the control. The selected term should persist across sessions. (3) All downstream calculations (getAffordablePrice, getBudgetProfile, calculateMortgageFromPayment) already accept `years` as a parameter and are correctly reactive — no logic changes needed. (4) Add term-year selector to `BudgetSlider` if it is present on the same page. Verify all payment scenarios recalculate in real-time when term changes.'>UI: Make loan term configurable on the Affordability Settings page. Currently <code>termYears</code> is hardcoded to 25 years in <code>useAffordability.ts</code> (useState(25)) and the Loan Term panel in <code>AffordabilitySettings.tsx</code> shows a static non-interactive placeholder (&#x27;Standard 25-Year Term&#x27;). (1) In <code>useAffordability.ts</code>: change <code>[termYears]</code> from <code>useState(25)</code> to <code>[termYears, setTermYears]</code> settable state. Persist <code>termYears</code> to localStorage under a <code>propSearch_loan_term</code> key. Expose <code>setTermYears</code> via an <code>updateTermYears</code> callback in the hook return. (2) In <code>AffordabilitySettings.tsx</code>: replace the static &#x27;25 Years&#x27; display in the Loan Term section (line 259) with an interactive segmented control or dropdown allowing the user to select from [15, 20, 25, 30] year terms. Wire <code>updateTermYears</code> to the control. The selected term should persist across sessions. (3) All downstream calculations (getAffordablePrice, getBudgetProfile, calculateMortgageFromPayment) already accept <code>years</code> as a parameter and are correctly reactive — no logic changes needed. (4) Add term-year selector to <code>BudgetSlider</code> if it is present on the same page. Verify all payment scenarios recalculate in real-time when term changes.</td><td>Todo</td><td>Frontend Engineer</td><td>None</td><td>2026-04-01</td></tr>
<tr><td><strong>FE-168</strong></td><td>High</td><td>Low</td><td style='max-width:480px;overflow:hidden;text-overflow:ellipsis;white-space:nowrap' title='UI Refactor: Make MortgageTracker (/mortgage) a read-only intelligence page. Per user directive, Mortgage Intelligence is purely for reading market data. All personal input controls (monthlyBudget, mortgageTerm, LTV band) belong exclusively on AffordabilitySettings (/affordability). Remove from MortgageTracker.tsx: (1) `const [monthlyBudget, setMonthlyBudget] = useState(4500)` and `const [mortgageTerm, setMortgageTerm] = useState(25)` state declarations (lines 34-35). (2) The Monthly Budget number input in the Global Control Bar (lines 155-169). (3) The Loan Term number input in the Global Control Bar (lines 170-184). (4) The non-functional &#x27;Execute Simulation&#x27; button (lines 576-578) — no onClick handler exists, dead UI. (5) Any `ppiHistory` calculations or `calculateMaxLoan` calls that depend on monthlyBudget/mortgageTerm — these personal affordability metrics belong on AffordabilitySettings. Keep: (a) LTV band selector (activeLTV) — this is a chart display filter for which market rate to show, not a personal setting. (b) Series visibility toggles (visibleSeries). Keep the Rate Corridor chart, PPI chart, and LTV Arbitrage Matrix — these display market data only. The charts should display the current live rate without any affordability personalisation. Update the page subtitle from &#x27;Purchasing Power Simulation&#x27; to &#x27;Market Rate Intelligence&#x27; or similar. Update the PPI chart label that references monthlyBudget (&#x27;Estimated Loan Ceiling @ £X/mo&#x27;) to show the current live 5yr fixed rate benchmark without personal budget inputs.'>UI Refactor: Make MortgageTracker (/mortgage) a read-only intelligence page. Per user directive, Mortgage Intelligence is purely for reading market data. All personal input controls (monthlyBudget, mortgageTerm, LTV band) belong exclusively on AffordabilitySettings (/affordability). Remove from MortgageTracker.tsx: (1) <code>const [monthlyBudget, setMonthlyBudget] = useState(4500)</code> and <code>const [mortgageTerm, setMortgageTerm] = useState(25)</code> state declarations (lines 34-35). (2) The Monthly Budget number input in the Global Control Bar (lines 155-169). (3) The Loan Term number input in the Global Control Bar (lines 170-184). (4) The non-functional &#x27;Execute Simulation&#x27; button (lines 576-578) — no onClick handler exists, dead UI. (5) Any <code>ppiHistory</code> calculations or <code>calculateMaxLoan</code> calls that depend on monthlyBudget/mortgageTerm — these personal affordability metrics belong on AffordabilitySettings. Keep: (a) LTV band selector (activeLTV) — this is a chart display filter for which market rate to show, not a personal setting. (b) Series visibility toggles (visibleSeries). Keep the Rate Corridor chart, PPI chart, and LTV Arbitrage Matrix — these display market data only. The charts should display the current live rate without any affordability personalisation. Update the page subtitle from &#x27;Purchasing Power Simulation&#x27; to &#x27;Market Rate Intelligence&#x27; or similar. Update the PPI chart label that references monthlyBudget (&#x27;Estimated Loan Ceiling @ £X/mo&#x27;) to show the current live 5yr fixed rate benchmark without personal budget inputs.</td><td>Todo</td><td>Frontend Engineer</td><td>None</td><td>2026-04-01</td></tr>

<tr><td><strong>QA-159</strong></td><td>High</td><td>Medium</td><td style='max-width:480px;overflow:hidden;text-overflow:ellipsis;white-space:nowrap' title='UI Tests failing — Dashboard: Alpha Score badge selector wrong. Dashboard.spec.ts line 39 uses `[class*=&#x27;alpha&#x27;]` which finds 0 elements because Tailwind compiles class names to color values (e.g. text-amber-400). Fix: update selector to target the badge container by text content (e.g. `th:has-text(&#x27;Alpha&#x27;), [class*=&#x27;AS&#x27;]`) or add data-testid to AlphaBadge component.'>UI Tests failing — Dashboard: Alpha Score badge selector wrong. Dashboard.spec.ts line 39 uses <code>[class*=&#x27;alpha&#x27;]</code> which finds 0 elements because Tailwind compiles class names to color values (e.g. text-amber-400). Fix: update selector to target the badge container by text content (e.g. <code>th:has-text(&#x27;Alpha&#x27;), [class*=&#x27;AS&#x27;]</code>) or add data-testid to AlphaBadge component.</td><td>Todo</td><td>Frontend Engineer</td><td>None</td><td>2026-04-01</td></tr>
<tr><td><strong>QA-160</strong></td><td>High</td><td>Medium</td><td style='max-width:480px;overflow:hidden;text-overflow:ellipsis;white-space:nowrap' title='UI Tests failing — MarketPulse.tsx silently renders nothing when macro data is null/error (line 24: `if (error || !data) return null`). This causes &#x27;Market Pulse&#x27; heading to be absent from the DOM entirely, breaking Dashboard test and the actual user-facing component. Fix: always render the header/loading skeleton even when data is unavailable; add graceful fallback UI instead of returning null.'>UI Tests failing — MarketPulse.tsx silently renders nothing when macro data is null/error (line 24: <code>if (error || !data) return null</code>). This causes &#x27;Market Pulse&#x27; heading to be absent from the DOM entirely, breaking Dashboard test and the actual user-facing component. Fix: always render the header/loading skeleton even when data is unavailable; add graceful fallback UI instead of returning null.</td><td>Todo</td><td>Frontend Engineer</td><td>None</td><td>2026-04-01</td></tr>
<tr><td><strong>QA-161</strong></td><td>Medium</td><td>Low</td><td style='max-width:480px;overflow:hidden;text-overflow:ellipsis;white-space:nowrap' title='UI Tests failing — ComparisonPage empty-state selectors don&#x27;t match actual UI. ComparisonPage.spec.ts line 23 expects regex `no.*properties|select.*properties|add.*compare` but the empty state shows &#x27;Get Started → Browse the Dashboard&#x27; CTA with no matching text. Also line 40 `alpha.*score|price.*efficiency|appreciation` fails because no properties = no matrix rows. Fix: update test selectors to match actual empty-state text, or restructure tests to first select properties before checking matrix.'>UI Tests failing — ComparisonPage empty-state selectors don&#x27;t match actual UI. ComparisonPage.spec.ts line 23 expects regex <code>no.*properties|select.*properties|add.*compare</code> but the empty state shows &#x27;Get Started → Browse the Dashboard&#x27; CTA with no matching text. Also line 40 <code>alpha.*score|price.*efficiency|appreciation</code> fails because no properties = no matrix rows. Fix: update test selectors to match actual empty-state text, or restructure tests to first select properties before checking matrix.</td><td>Todo</td><td>Frontend Engineer</td><td>None</td><td>2026-04-01</td></tr>
<tr><td><strong>QA-163</strong></td><td>Low</td><td>Low</td><td style='max-width:480px;overflow:hidden;text-overflow:ellipsis;white-space:nowrap' title='UI Tests failing — Dashboard &#x27;property count&#x27; test (Dashboard.spec.ts line 62) looks for `\d+\s*(properties|assets)` text which does not exist in the dashboard UI. <tr><td><strong>DAT-154</strong></td><td>High</td><td>Low</td><td style='max-width:480px;overflow:hidden;text-overflow:ellipsis;white-space:nowrap' title='Data: Establish automated backup protocol for `data/` directory — create `data/backups/` directory, implement dated snapshot script, maintain `data/backups/LOG.md`, enforce weekly + pre-operation snapshot discipline per analyst README.'>Data: Establish automated backup protocol for <code>data/</code> directory — create <code>data/backups/</code> directory, implement dated snapshot script, maintain <code>data/backups/LOG.md</code>, enforce weekly + pre-operation snapshot discipline per analyst README.</td><td>Done</td><td>Data Analyst</td><td>None</td><td>2026-03-30</td></tr>
<tr><td><strong>DAT-157</strong></td><td>Medium</td><td>Medium</td><td style='max-width:480px;overflow:hidden;text-overflow:ellipsis;white-space:nowrap' title='Historical mortgage rates lack 2yr/5yr term split. The `mortgage_history` table in SQLite only contains rates by LTV band (`rate_90`, `rate_85`, `rate_75`, `rate_60`) but no term length differentiation (2yr vs 5yr). Currently `mortgage_2yr` uses `rate_90` as a proxy which conflates LTV band with term length. Source 2yr and 5yr historical rates by LTV band from BoE Effective Interest Rates database: https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/boeapps/database/. Populate `mortgage_history` with `{ date, boe_rate, mortgage_2yr_90ltv, mortgage_5yr_75ltv, ... }`. See DAT-131.'>Historical mortgage rates lack 2yr/5yr term split. The <code>mortgage_history</code> table in SQLite only contains rates by LTV band (<code>rate_90</code>, <code>rate_85</code>, <code>rate_75</code>, <code>rate_60</code>) but no term length differentiation (2yr vs 5yr). Currently <code>mortgage_2yr</code> uses <code>rate_90</code> as a proxy which conflates LTV band with term length. Source 2yr and 5yr historical rates by LTV band from BoE Effective Interest Rates database: https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/boeapps/database/. Populate <code>mortgage_history</code> with <code>{ date, boe_rate, mortgage_2yr_90ltv, mortgage_5yr_75ltv, ... }</code>. See DAT-131.</td><td>Todo</td><td>Data Analyst</td><td>None</td><td>2026-03-30</td></tr>

As of 2026-04-01, the auto-archive/purge policy has been replaced by an `archived` flag on `properties`. All 25 records previously in `archived_properties` have been restored to `properties` with `archived=1` and `archive_reason=&#x27;Shallow data: Needs Enrichment&#x27;`. **Enrich all 25 flagged records**: source missing EPC ratings, sqft, and other missing fields via re-scrape. Once enriched, set `archived=0`. Records without valid addresses (1 null-address record: `ab5c0faa-b051-414d-a427-bf7ba4c39d5d`) should be investigated — if they cannot be enriched, set `archived=1` with `archive_reason=&#x27;Cannot Verify — Discard&#x27;` (do not delete). Once enriched, set <code>archived=0</code>. Records without valid addresses (1 null-address record: <code>ab5c0faa-b051-414d-a427-bf7ba4c39d5d</code>) should be investigated — if they cannot be enriched, set <code>archived=1</code> with <code>archive_reason=&#x27;Cannot Verify — Discard&#x27;</code> (do not delete). <tr><td><strong>DE-162</strong></td><td>High</td><td>Medium</td><td style='max-width:480px;overflow:hidden;text-overflow:ellipsis;white-space:nowrap' title='Data: Establish automated weekly data refresh pipeline. Current state: zero scheduled jobs — `make sync` is manual-only, `macro_trend.json` last refreshed 2026-03-30 (2 days ago). (1) Add a GitHub Actions cron job (cron: &#x27;0 9 * * 1&#x27; — every Monday 09:00 UTC) to run `make sync` and commit the updated data. (2) Add a cron job to refresh `macro_trend.json` with latest BoE rate, HPI, and market volume data on the same weekly schedule. (3) Add a freshness indicator to the dashboard: in MarketPulse, show days-since-last-refresh with a red/amber/green indicator if data is &gt;7/&gt;3/&gt;0 days old. (4) Add `_last_full_refresh` and `_next_expected_refresh` timestamps to the API response (`GET /api/macro`). (5) Document the schedule in the Data Analyst README under Backup Protocol.'>Data: Establish automated weekly data refresh pipeline. Current state: zero scheduled jobs — <code>make sync</code> is manual-only, <code>macro_trend.json</code> last refreshed 2026-03-30 (2 days ago). (1) Add a GitHub Actions cron job (cron: &#x27;0 9 * * 1&#x27; — every Monday 09:00 UTC) to run <code>make sync</code> and commit the updated data. (2) Add a cron job to refresh <code>macro_trend.json</code> with latest BoE rate, HPI, and market volume data on the same weekly schedule. (3) Add a freshness indicator to the dashboard: in MarketPulse, show days-since-last-refresh with a red/amber/green indicator if data is &gt;7/&gt;3/&gt;0 days old. (4) Add <code>_last_full_refresh</code> and <code>_next_expected_refresh</code> timestamps to the API response (<code>GET /api/macro</code>). (5) Document the schedule in the Data Analyst README under Backup Protocol.</td><td>Todo</td><td>Data Engineer</td><td>None</td><td>2026-04-01</td></tr>
<tr><td><strong>DE-162</strong></td><td>High</td><td>Medium</td><td style='max-width:480px;overflow:hidden;text-overflow:ellipsis;white-space:nowrap' title='Tag genuine sold records in archived_properties. The 25 records in archived_properties are all pre-enrichment duplicates (archive_reason=Shallow data: Needs Enrichment) — they are NOT genuinely sold. The regional_velocity view now correctly excludes them (sold_count=0 everywhere). To get meaningful sold/signal data: (1) manually review archived_properties and set archive_reason to a sold status (Sold STC / Completed) for genuinely sold records; (2) optionally move enrichment-complete records back to properties with archived=0; (3) once genuine sold records exist, regional_velocity supply_ratio and market_signal will become meaningful. Current state: sold_count=0 across all areas.'>Tag genuine sold records in archived_properties. The 25 records in archived_properties are all pre-enrichment duplicates (archive_reason=Shallow data: Needs Enrichment) — they are NOT genuinely sold. The regional_velocity view now correctly excludes them (sold_count=0 everywhere). To get meaningful sold/signal data: (1) manually review archived_properties and set archive_reason to a sold status (Sold STC / Completed) for genuinely sold records; (2) optionally move enrichment-complete records back to properties with archived=0; (3) once genuine sold records exist, regional_velocity supply_ratio and market_signal will become meaningful. Current state: sold_count=0 across all areas.</td><td>Todo</td><td>Data Analyst</td><td>None</td><td>2026-04-01</td></tr>
